reviews.review_title: Not too easy, not too hard
reviews.review_description: I’m a big fan of escape room style games. However, some ER games are really too easy and some seem impossibly hard that you get stuck and can’t finish the game. Oldest Golden Treasure is somewhere between easy and medium difficulty. The puzzles are all unique, range from easy to medium in complexity, and differ enough to keep things fresh. This is not a game from a big budget studio but you can tell the dev put a lot of thought into the puzzles. I’m a big fan of indie developers and I also own their previous game as well. Like my title says, it’s not too easy and also not too hard. If you enjoy escape room style games, I would check this one out.

reviews.review_title: Solid Puzzler with lots of extras
reviews.review_description: I really enjoy these types of puzzle games. Not too hard, a decent hints option, the historical documents and coins collecting are nice extras. I appreciate the two modes of movement (teleport or 'walking'), the progress notebook and the clean graphics. I'm not too far into it yet but so far there are no issues and it's very fun! Thanks very much!

reviews.review_title: Great introduction to the puzzler genre
reviews.review_description:
If you're new to puzzle escape games this is a perfect game to get familiar with the genre.  It's not so hard and frustrating that you can't get through it and it offers hints if you get stuck.  

This is an independent project that the developer spent a good bit of time and resources to build out.  

I hope they add to it with updates like the creator of Metacity Patrol.  It's been fascinating seeing the development over time as they add to their own skill set and apply it adding to the user experience.  

I would recommend this app for anyone looking for a casual puzzler that's not too frustrating and difficult.  I look forward to seeing future experiences created by this developer.

developer_response.body:
Hello! Thank you for your feedback! I am a solo developer and I am sorry that you felt that the development was rushed. I have spent almost 2 years working on this game and this is the best I can do. I wanted to have a good hint system, a story and more niche puzzles/interactions. I definitely have not planned the game to be meant for kids. I am currently working on the next big update for the game and will continue to add more content.

In the meantime you can always try to uncover some of the easter eggs, collect all hidden coins or try to optimize your solution time and do a speedrun.

If you have any suggestions on how I can improve the game please join my discord. I am always happy to hear new ideas and have already made many improvements thanks to the active community :)

reviews.review_description:
I quite enjoyed my time playing this story puzzler.
The atmosphere is good, story is good and interesting…. and the sounds and graphics are decent.

Puzzles arnt too challenging, but are still fun and get you thinking.
I like the interesting history facts you find scattered about too, which add to the story.

There is a couple of issues stopping it from being a golden treasure of a puzzler… I threw one item and it vanished up through the ceiling and manipulation of some items needs to be better… also a crouch button would be good. 

It’s a decent puzzler and I will return to it… there’s some better ones out there, but there’s way more worse puzzlers.

If you like puzzlers, I’d recommend this game.

The graphics were very good.  Story line and plot was very intriguing!  Learned a lot of interesting facts about Bulgaria. There were a few glitches with holding items, but nothing that couldn't be fixed by dropping the item and picking it back up.  Hoping to see a sequel!

As a person who loves playing puzzle games in VR in this game the puzzles are quite clever and not too tricky, just right in terms of challenge and ease of play. Every new environment brings something fresh to the table, keeping things interesting and always changing. The game also has many historical facts which I find very interesting, because I have never seen anything like this done before. I would highly recommend it to anyone who is searching for something unique to play!
This is the second challenging puzzle / escape room game made by Vasil! This time it has an interesting plot and is improved in all aspects comparing to the debut. Highly reccomend!

I beta tested Oldest Golden Treasure and overall I enjoyed my time with it. Performance is very good for an indie VR game — it runs smoothly and looks solid in VR. Some of the puzzles, especially earlier on, were genuinely fun and well thought out, and the house/basement sections had good pacing and momentum.

The main issues for me were polish and consistency. I ran into a number of bugs and glitches during my playthrough, which occasionally broke immersion. The story also started off interesting but felt rushed and a bit unfocused toward the end, making the conclusion less satisfying than it could have been.

That said, the developer has already been actively fixing issues based on feedback, which is really encouraging. There’s clearly a good game here, and with continued updates, stronger atmosphere, and refinement, this could become something special.

FIRST-PERSON EXPLORATION AND MYSTERY
Oldest Golden Treasure successfully establishes an intriguing atmosphere from the very first minutes: you're immersed in a world of puzzles, hidden clues, and immersive locations. 
Exploring underground tunnels, ruins, and secret passages immediately makes you want to go further to understand "what happened.”
VARIED AND WELL-THOUGHT-OUT PUZZLES
The puzzles are well-designed: neither too simple nor completely obscure. 
They require genuine observation and logical thinking, which enhances immersion.
Traveling “through time” also adds an interesting narrative dimension and gives depth to the story. 
SOLID TECHNIQUE FOR AN INDIE VR GAME 
For a solo project, the game offers a smooth and polished experience. 
The environments are clear, the interactions feel natural, and there's a clear attempt to balance adventure and accessibility. 
It's a real pleasure to explore the scenes and solve the challenges without excessive frustration.
CONCLUSION  
Oldest Golden Treasure is an immersive VR adventure that combines puzzle-solving, exploration, and storytelling in a mysterious and engaging world. 
Whether you enjoy escape rooms or stories to uncover, this title definitely deserves your attention!

developer_response.body: Thank you for such a detailed and thoughtful review! I’m happy you enjoyed the puzzles, exploration, and immersive atmosphere. This kind of support truly motivates me as a solo indie dev!

reviews.review_title: An escape room game for everyone
reviews.review_description:
I usually don't enjoy puzzle games, but this one actually hooked me. The game feels really smooth and I haven't run into any bugs that ruined the experience. The story is well designed and the hint feature is great because it "tunnels" you in the right direction without spoiling the solution.
A really nice touch is the movement. You can teleport around and grab things from far away instead of having to walk everywhere, which makes the gameplay feel a lot better in VR. I also love the history facts hidden around the maps. This is the second game I've played from this dev and it’s definitely worth trying out!

If Indiana Jones and a VR escape room had a shiny little baby, it would be Oldest Golden Treasure. This game nails that “one more puzzle, then I’ll stop” feeling, and suddenly it’s 2 a.m. and you’re pointing at clues like a caffeine-fueled detective in a headset.

What I loved:
	•	Puzzles that feel clever, not cruel: You get satisfying “AHA!” moments without needing a PhD in Moon Runes.
	•	Atmosphere for days: The world has that ancient, mysterious vibe that makes you slow down and actually look around, because everything feels suspicious (in a good way).
	•	Great pacing: It keeps feeding you new discoveries so you stay curious, not stuck.

If you like treasure hunts, puzzle-solving, and exploring places you absolutely should not touch in real life, this is a must-play.

Highly recommended.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the detailed and well-thought-out review! I’m really glad you enjoyed the puzzles and atmosphere. The Indiana Jones + VR escape room comparison made my day 😄
I truly appreciate the recommendation!

reviews.review_title: Fun Indie Escape Room Puzzle Adventure!
reviews.review_description: I had a blast playing this. I love escape rooms, but prefer the ones that break out of the ordinary puzzles. The puzzles in this game are genuinely unique and just challenging enough. I only had to consult the hints a couple of times. The hints are well structured and give you just as much help as you need, escalating to a full solve if you just aren't getting it (only had to do that once). If you love indie games, this one is a gem, give it a go!

I am playing the game and so far I love it, it is very enjoyable. The puzzles are great and there are hints just in case you missed something. I enjoy the quest very much , it keeps you interested in the storyline. 
Great game, very well thought out. Also i enjoy the historical facts too, those are very interesting to learn about. 
It is a very good game , i hope more people try it ☺️💖💖💖

developer_response.body: Thank you for the very positive and friendly review! I am very happy that you liked my game :)

reviews.review_description: A decently fun game with a good mix of familiarity and uniqueness. It feels like an escape room in the beginning, but that quickly evolves into a puzzle adventure. Would love to see a follow-up or extra content connected in the same universe.

reviews.review_title: A Clever and Enjoyable VR Puzzler
reviews.review_description:
This is a genuinely enjoyable VR escape-room experience. The puzzles themselves are well-designed: fair in difficulty, varied enough to avoid repetition, and satisfying to crack without ever feeling frustrating. And if you get really stuck there are 3 hints which are granted after short time waits…
The movement and interaction felt ok both smooth and responsive, and there are several setting for moving from teleport to locomotion, graphically the game looks great for a solo developer and runs nicely with environments which although are basic, are quite immersive and help sell the sense of discovery.  But I did find some glitching such as clipping when placing items down they would sometimes go lower and stick out of tables and draws making them hard to re grab.
I believe the dev is proactive and will continue to patch and improve the game and will revisit improve my score as and when the game is updated.

Let me preface by saying I got this game for free for the purpose of beta testing
The game was enjoyable and the puzzles were not too difficult, only had to use hints a couple times. I recommend to keep a watchful eye for any interact able props or buttons.
For me my first run took a couple-ish hours but there is also a speedrun mode with a leaderboard if that your thing and incentivizes multiple playthroughs. Also teleporting is WAY faster than just using the joystick as there is no sprint button so your stuck only walking

reviews.review_title: Challenging puzzle game by a solo dev
reviews.review_description: This is a challenging puzzle / escape room game with an interesting plot made by a solo dev. Stages are varied and the puzzles are fair and not annoying. I wish it could be a little bit longer though. Overall, if you're looking for a puzzle / escape room game with speedruns, I suggest you try this out and support a solo dev.

Find clues and solve riddles so you can repair the timemachine. 
Found your way out of the cave and the dungeons.

When you have finished the game, you can do the speedrun, as fast as you can.
Search for all the hidden coins.

Graphics are great.  If they are gonna add some more noises/rumors in the cave, that would be great.
Was one of the lucky few to test the game before hand and really enjoyed it. If you enjoyed Dark Matter then this one is definitely for you. Highly recommended
Loved this one! Nice variety of puzzles. Really neat graphics. Fun exploration. I love the time machine aspect and operation. Very cool!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the nice and positive review! I am very happy you have enjoyed the game! I will continue to improve it and add more stuff in future updates :)

reviews.review_title: A Great New Puzzle Escape Experience
reviews.review_description:
This looks actually quite good, graphically very sharp, nice concept, I love a detective story adventure, not too complicated at the moment, has a nice flow to the puzzles, the thundering is a nice touch in the first level
I have spoken to the dev, requested more sounds and music and he is working on this, so this should add even more atmosphere.
So far so good, puzzle fans will love it.

There are no jumpscares or anything else scary in the game. I was worried because there are many dark scenes, but aside from some lightning sounds during the rain that may startle you a little bit, the game is very comfortable.
The game is perfect and it's really fun it tests your brain power and raises your problem solving skills it's fun and worth it best purchase oat.
I’m usually not a fan of these type games but this one was decent
